questions.question: Explain the concept of coprocesses in Unix and how they differ from regular background jobs. Give an example syntax.
keyPoints: Coprocess provides bidirectional communication channel
Background jobs (&) only redirect stdout
Coprocess uses pipe file descriptors for read/write
Syntax: coproc NAME { command; }
Variables: NAME[0], NAME[1], NAME_PID

questions.question: Write a shell script that uses a coprocess to create a calculator using bc command. Demonstrate sending expressions and reading results.
keyPoints: Create coproc BC with bc -l
Write expressions to BC[1]
Read results from BC[0]
Close write end after use
Wait for process and handle exit status

questions.question: What are the potential problems when using coprocesses with interactive programs? How can they be mitigated?
keyPoints: Buffering causes delayed responses
stdbuf or unbuffer can force line buffering
Process may hang if EOF not sent
Close write end to signal completion
disown prevents 'Done' messages

questions.question: Explain the file descriptor arrangement in a named coprocess. How does the shell set up the communication channels?
keyPoints: Shell creates pipe pair for stdin/stdout
One end assigned to shell's array (NAME[0], NAME[1])
Other end duplicated to coprocess's stdin/stdout
NAME_PID stores child process ID
Original file descriptors closed in parent

questions.question: Compare using FIFOs with coprocesses versus anonymous pipes. When would you choose each method?
keyPoints: FIFOs are named pipes - persist in filesystem
Anonymous pipes (default coproc) are temporary
FIFOs allow multiple writers/readers
FIFOs enable unrelated process communication
Choose anonymous for simple shell scripts, FIFOs for complex IPC

questions.question: Design and implement a client-server architecture using coprocesses in Unix. The server should maintain state and respond to multiple client requests through named FIFOs.
keyPoints: Create input FIFO and output FIFO using mkfifo
Server coproc reads from input FIFO, writes to output FIFO
Parent creates both FIFOs before starting coproc
Handle multiple requests by parsing commands
Maintain state (e.g., running total, history)
Proper cleanup of FIFOs and processes
Error handling for missing FIFOs
Use trap for cleanup on exit

questions.question: Write a comprehensive program that demonstrates all aspects of coprocess management: creation, bidirectional communication, signal handling, and proper cleanup. Include error handling and edge cases.
keyPoints: Named coproc with proper variable naming
Read/write with proper file descriptor management
Signal handling (SIGHUP, SIGTERM)
Proper EOF signaling by closing write end
Wait for process and capture exit status
Disown to prevent job control messages
Timeout handling for blocking reads
Resource cleanup (close FDs, remove FIFOs)
